channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy concerned as U.S. suspends Russia sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: FBI investigates attacks in Michigan and Virginia. And, Senate passes housing bill
item.description: The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.
item.content:encoded:
The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.

(Image credit: Emily Elconin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel launches airstrikes in Beirut, displacing thousands of people
item.description: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.content:encoded: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: CNN correspondent shares his takeaways from his recent visit to Iran
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: U.S. military bombs Iran's main oil export hub, as Mideast war toll mounts
item.description: President Trump said on Friday the U.S. military had "totally obliterated every MILITARY target in Iran's crown jewel, Kharg Island." This, as all six crew members on a refueling plane that went down in western Iraq were confirmed dead.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said on Friday the U.S. military had "totally obliterated every MILITARY target in Iran's crown jewel, Kharg Island." This, as all six crew members on a refueling plane that went down in western Iraq were confirmed dead.

(Image credit: Kawnat Haju)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Exiled Iranians watch the war from afar, wondering what awaits when it ends
item.description: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.
item.content:encoded: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
item.description: A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.
item.content:encoded:
A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.

(Image credit: Odelyn Joseph)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China slams Trump's trade investigation, as it approves a 5-year economic plan
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.
item.content:encoded:
China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
item.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
item.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.content:encoded: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas
